pattern: A monotonic stack lets each element inherit how many rounds its predecessors needed, so the deletion process collapses to one pass.
hints: Simulating every deletion round is too slow; think about how many rounds each element survives before a larger left neighbour eats it.
An element is removed in the round determined by the elements to its left that are larger than it; a decreasing stack can carry this timing.

Walk left to right with a stack holding (value, rounds-needed). For each value, pop smaller-or-equal tops while tracking the max rounds seen; the current element's round count is that max plus one (or zero if the stack empties). The answer is the largest round count.

edge_inputs.description: strictly decreasing removes all but the first in one round
a deep valley delays a later removal
peak then descending chain takes several rounds

stress.description: large array over a narrow value range to force long deletion chains
